subject: Sociology
title: Mass Incarceration
description: Learn Mass Incarceration in one minute.

hook.text: The U.S. has 4% of the world's people and 25% of its prisoners. Black men are imprisoned at five times the white rate.

buildup.text: Mass incarceration exploded after the 1980s War on Drugs, which targeted Black communities disproportionately.

discovery.text: Michelle Alexander calls it the New Jim Crow: a legal system that strips rights from millions of Black citizens.

twist.text: Drug use rates are similar across races. But Black people are arrested, convicted, and sentenced at far higher rates.

climax.text: A felony conviction means lost voting rights, denied housing, and blocked jobs. Prison doesn't end at the gate.

punchline.text: Equal crimes, unequal punishment. That's the system working as built.

quiz.question: What did Michelle Alexander call mass incarceration?
options: A temporary policy that ended in the 1990s
